name: Dr. Thivya Kandappu
bio: Thivya leads PEARL Lab. She received her PhD from University of New South Wales. Her expertise lies at the intersection of pervasive computing, privacy-preserving mobile sensing, and human-centered analytics. Her interdisciplinary research bridges methodological innovation with real-world applications, making significant contributions to smart city systems, wearable technologies, and cognitive health. Her current focus areas include event-based vision, context-aware systems for aging, and technology-enhanced learning for individualized cognitive assessment.

name: Gevindu Ganganath
bio: I am a doctoral researcher specializing in Human-Computer Interaction and AI. My work in Educational AI focuses on developing LLM-powered tutoring agents that foster student engagement. By combining multimodal sensing like EEG and eye-tracking with adaptive pedagogical strategies, I aim to create intelligent systems that respond dynamically to a learner's cognitive and emotional states.

name: Yiming Zhang
bio: Yiming Zhang is a Research Scientist specializing in Wearable/Mobile Health, AIOT and Health Informatics. Expertise spans large-scale clinical data acquisition, biomedical data analysis, interpretable/personalized AI/ML, edge deployment, and wearable device prototyping.

name: Thamilezai Ananthakumar
bio: Visiting Undergraduate Research Student in Human–Machine Collaborative Systems and Pervasive Sensing. Research focuses on advancing photoplethysmography for wearable healthcare applications. Interests include biomedical instrumentation, wearable device design, and bio signal processing, including AI-driven methods for robust real-world health monitoring systems.

name: Vedic Chawla
bio: I am a Visiting Research Student at SMU and an Undergraduate Student at IIIT Surat. I have previously contributed to Apache Datafusion, an open-source database query engine framework, and authored FedAsyncM, a hybrid aggregation algorithm for Federated Learning. I am currently working on Event-Stream Super Resolution at SMU, with the aim to capture high-speed subtle motion more clearly. Beyond that, my interests span Intelligent Systems and Learning Algorithms.
